context.note: First recorded baseline. The reduction sprint (features #1-#43) did not capture a pre-reduction snapshot, so no regression comparison is possible. This file records the post-reduction floor against which future drift is measured.

methodology.rationale: In-session execution. The eval_runner default executor (claude -p subprocess) hangs when invoked from inside Claude Code (parent process holds locks the child needs). Task-tool dispatch is the documented alternative — see eval_runner.py docstring lines 6-7. For external CI re-runs use: python -m platxa_agent_generator.cli --json eval-run --all --save-history (note: --json is a top-level parent-parser flag and must precede the subcommand).
methodology.scenario_filter: one correctness_01.yaml per category (6 categories, axis=correctness)

threshold.rationale: Capability scenarios are advisory (evaluate_exit returns 0 even on failure). The 80% threshold imposes an explicit quality floor without re-typing every scenario to regression.

limitations.scope: 6 of 60 scenarios (one per category, correctness axis only). Remaining 54 scenarios (clarity, completeness, robustness, scope_adherence, security, tool_design axes) deferred to external CI run.
limitations.comparison_point: None. No pre-reduction baseline was captured; this file IS the v1 baseline for all future comparisons.
limitations.executor_substitution: Task tool with general-purpose subagent_type substitutes for the default claude -p subprocess. The general-purpose subagent has slightly richer context (CLAUDE.md, available skills/agents) than a bare claude -p invocation, which may inflate pass rate vs the external runner. Re-running externally is recommended to capture the apples-to-apples baseline.

next_steps: Run python -m platxa_agent_generator.cli --json eval-run --all --save-history > .claude/evals/baseline-external-YYYY-MM-DD.json from an external shell (not inside a Claude Code session) to capture the full 60-scenario baseline (--json must precede the subcommand).
Once captured, treat this in-session baseline as a smoke test and the external run as the authoritative figure.
Re-run after any agent definition, skill reference, or prompt-template change.
